content.text: Find all SQLAlchemy models in the codebase and list their relationships, then refactor the User model to use proper relationship backpopulation
This is a two-part task. I should use a sub-agent to explore the codebase for models while I plan the refactoring.
I'll launch a sub-agent to explore the codebase for all SQLAlchemy models while I plan the refactoring approach.

input.prompt: Find all SQLAlchemy model classes in the codebase. List each model with its table name, columns, and relationships.

content.text: Found 3 models: User (users table), Post (posts table), Comment (comments table). User has posts relationship but no backref. Post has comments relationship with cascade delete.

content.content:
src/models/user.py:15: posts = relationship('Post')
src/models/post.py:12: comments = relationship('Comment', cascade='all, delete-orphan')
src/models/post.py:13: author = relationship('User', back_populates='posts')

content.text: The sub-agent found that `User.posts` is missing `back_populates`. `Post.author` already references `User` with `back_populates='posts'`, but the User side doesn't reciprocate. Let me fix that.
